page.content: The following table presents key serological and genetic markers related to hepatitis B virus (HBV) infection, specifically focusing on the metrics of Anti-HBs (mIU/mL) and Anti-HBc (s/CO) among a cohort with distinct genotypic variations. Understanding the serological landscape of HBV is crucial for assessing the immune response to infection and vaccination, as well as for evaluating the risk of developing chronic liver disease. In this dataset, the measured levels of Anti-HBs at 119.87 mIU/mL suggest a potential protective immunity, indicative of either successful vaccination or past infection resolution. Conversely, the Anti-HBc level of 4.15 s/CO indicates ongoing or past exposure to the virus, as this marker is typically associated with HBV infection history.

page.content: Additionally, the presence of specific genetic markers such as the Precore/Core Gene, Surface Gene, Polymerase Gene, and X Gene can provide insights into the viral load and mutation patterns that may affect treatment approaches. The demographic details, including the sex of the participant (Male), further contextualize the interpretation of these serological and genetic findings within the broader epidemiological framework of HBV infection. This comprehensive analysis is essential for informing clinical decisions and public health strategies targeting HBV management.
